task_completion.description: Verify that Microsoft Word is open with the correct raw requirements document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Word application is visible, and the title bar shows 'fms_requirements_raw.docx'.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Heading 1' style is applied to the first top-level section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text '1. INTRODUCTION' is visibly formatted as a Heading 1 (typically larger, distinct font). The Word Styles ribbon should show 'Heading 1' highlighted when the cursor is on this text, or it should appear as a top-level item in the Navigation Pane.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Heading 2' style is applied to the initial numbered sub-sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text '1.1 Purpose' and '1.2 Scope' are formatted as Heading 2. They should appear nested under '1. INTRODUCTION' in the Word Navigation Pane, or the Styles ribbon should show 'Heading 2' selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Heading 1' style is applied to all eight numbered top-level sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Word Navigation Pane shows an outline with eight top-level sections, starting with '1. INTRODUCTION' and ending with '8. INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS', all at the same highest outline level.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Heading 2' style is applied to all other numbered sub-sections throughout the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Word Navigation Pane shows sub-sections properly nested under their respective top-level headings (e.g., '2.1 Product Perspective', '3.1 Position Determination Requirements', '4.1 Takeoff Performance', '5.1 Flight Plan Entry and Modification').

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Microsoft Word graphical user interface to apply styles.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Word ribbon being clicked, the Styles gallery being opened, or the Navigation pane being used, rather than a terminal script modifying the docx file.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual 'fms_requirements_raw.docx' file rather than creating a new blank document and typing the headings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The document contains the original unformatted SRS body text (e.g., RTM entries, Collins Aerospace proprietary text, FAR Part 25 references) alongside the newly formatted headings.

integrity.description: The agent applied actual Word styles rather than just manually changing font sizes and making text bold.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Navigation Pane correctly builds a document outline based on the applied styles. Manual font size changes do not populate the Navigation Pane.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document being edited must be 'fms_requirements_raw.docx' for the FMS-4000 Flight Management System. It contains 18 Requirements Traceability Matrix entries (RTM-001 through RTM-018), Collins Aerospace proprietary documentation, and regulatory references (FAR Part 25, EASA CS-25). The task requires applying 'Heading 1' to exactly 8 top-level sections (1. INTRODUCTION through 8. INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS) and 'Heading 2' to all numbered sub-sections (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, 3.1, 4.1, 5.1). The most reliable way to verify this visually is by checking the Microsoft Word Navigation Pane for a properly nested outline.
